title: Hadoop MapReduce Cluster Architecture
description: The Hadoop MapReduce cluster architecture comprises a JobTracker, TaskTrackers (now replaced by NodeManagers in YARN), and a distributed file system (HDFS). The JobTracker coordinates and monitors all jobs, while NodeManagers manage resources and task execution on each node. MapReduce jobs process data in parallel across the cluster, with the Map phase distributing data processing tasks and the Reduce phase aggregating results using the ValueAggregator library for custom aggregation. This architecture ensures scalability, reliability, and fault tolerance through data replication in HDFS and task tracking by the JobTracker. The use of Java for development and Hadoop APIs for interaction with the distributed file system and job management underscores the technology stack. The coordinator pattern is evident in the JobTracker's role, while the pipeline pattern is observed in the Map-Reduce workflow.

TITLE: Hadoop MapReduce Cluster Architecture

DESCRIPTION: The Hadoop MapReduce cluster architecture comprises a JobTracker, TaskTrackers (now replaced by NodeManagers in YARN), and a distributed file system (HDFS). The JobTracker coordinates and monitors all jobs, while NodeManagers manage resources and task execution on each node. MapReduce jobs process data in parallel across the cluster, with the Map phase distributing data processing tasks and the Reduce phase aggregating results using the ValueAggregator library for custom aggregation. This architecture ensures scalability, reliability, and fault tolerance through data replication in HDFS and task tracking by the JobTracker. The use of Java for development and Hadoop APIs for interaction with the distributed file system and job management underscores the technology stack. The coordinator pattern is evident in the JobTracker's role, while the pipeline pattern is observed in the Map-Reduce workflow.
